name: zoxide (Smart cd Command)

performance.notes: Rust-based; frecency database lookup; negligible overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Smart directory jumper using frecency (frequency + recency). `z project` jumps to most frequent/recent directory matching 'project'. `zi` for interactive fzf selection. Works with bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell, nushell. Replaces z, autojump, fasd. Shell integration: eval '$(zoxide init zsh)'. zi uses fzf for fuzzy selection. Much faster and more accurate than autojump. Tracks visits, learns patterns. `z -` for previous directory. Used with alias cd='z' pattern.

gotchas: Interactive TUI — not for non-interactive scripts
New directories don't appear until visited
Limited value until frequently-used directory patterns established
best_when: Developer workstations with many project directories
Pair with fzf for interactive directory selection (zi)
Replacing autojump/z for more accurate frecency-based navigation
summary: zoxide is a smart cd command that learns your most-used directories via frecency (frequency + recency). `z project` jumps to the best matching directory. `zi` for interactive fzf selection. Rust-based, faster and more accurate than autojump/z. Works with bash, zsh, fish, nushell, PowerShell. Standard in modern developer dotfiles alongside fzf, bat, and ripgrep.
